title: fix: update Sirius elevator app key (old key deactivated)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
The Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge login broke — the `/authorize` consent screen showed **"This app key could not be verified. Authorization blocked."**

**Root cause:** the registered app key `pk_vbqLj6cwn05D2v5B` no longer resolves. `app-lookup` returns `{"found":false}`, which triggers that exact error in `authorize.tsx`.

**Fix:** swap to the re-issued key `pk_nDXh3ryXfjTkirMB`. Verified against the live endpoint:
- old key → `{"found":false}`
- new key → `{"found":true}`, app `"Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge"`, owner `voodoohop`, redirect allowlist includes `sirius-cybernetics-elevator-challenge.pollinations.ai`, `earningsEnabled:true`

One-line change in `apps/sirius-cybernetics-elevator-challenge/src/hooks/ui.ts`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Elevator doors reopening
gist.blurb: Fixed a broken login key for the Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge app. The doors are open and ready for your prompts again!
gist.summary: Swapped the deactivated app key for a newly issued key in the Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge to fix the broken /authorize consent screen.
gist.impact: Users can successfully log into the Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge again instead of hitting an 'app key could not be verified' error.
gist.visual_concept: A retro-futuristic elevator with doors opening. Polly the bee wearing a tiny bellhop hat, fixing the elevator panel with a glowing key.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a retro-futuristic elevator with its doors sliding open. The cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bellhop hat is inserting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) key into the elevator's control panel. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
